Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2024-11-01 section=business period_end=2024-09-28
Item 1.    Business
Company Background
The Company designs, manufactures and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables and accessories, and sells a variety of related services. The Company's fiscal year is the 52- or 53-week p
…[truncated 7445 chars]…
g fluctuations.
The Company uses some custom components that are not commonly used by its competitors, and new products introduced by the Company often utilize custom components available from only one source. When a component or product uses new technologies, initial capacity constraints may exist until the suppliers' yields have matured or their manufacturing capacities have increased. The continued availability of these components at acceptable prices, or at all, may be affected if suppliers decide to concentrate on the production of common components instead of components customized to meet the Company's requirements.
The Company has entered into agreements for the supply of many components; however, there can be no guarantee that the Company will be able to extend or renew these agreements on similar terms, or at all.
Research and Development
Because the industries in which the Company competes are characterized by rapid technological advances, the Company's ability to compete successfully depends heavily upon its ability to ensure a continual and timely flow of competitive products, services and technologies to the marketplace. The Company continues to develop new technologies to enhance existing products and services, and to expand the range of its offerings through research and development ("R&D"), licensing of intellectual property and acquisition of third-party businesses and technology.
Intellectual Property
The Company currently holds a broad collection of intellectual property rights relating to certain aspects of its hardware, accessories, software and services. This includes patents, designs, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets and other forms of intellectual property rights in the U.S. and various foreign countries. Although the Company believes the ownership of such intellectual property rights is an important factor in differentiating its business and that its success does depend in part on such ownership, the Company relies primarily on the innovative skills, technical competence and marketing abilities of its personnel.
The Company regularly files patent, design, copyright and trademark applications to protect innovations arising from its research, development, design and marketing, and is currently pursuing thousands of applications around the world. Over time, the Company has accumulated a large portfolio of issued and registered intellectual property rights around the world. No single intellectual property right is solely responsible for protecting the Company's products and services. The Company believes the duration of its intellectual property rights is adequate relative to the expected lives of its products and services.
In addition to Company-owned intellectual property, many of the Company's products and services are designed to include intellectual property owned by third parties. It may be necessary in the future to seek or renew licenses relating to various aspects of the Company's products, processes and services. While the Company has generally been able to obtain such licenses on commercially reasonable terms in the past, there is no guarantee that such licenses could be obtained in the future on reasonable terms or at all.
Apple Inc. | 2024 Form 10-K | 3
Business Seasonality and Product Introductions
The Company has historically experienced higher net sales in its first quarter compared to other quarters in its fiscal year due in part to seasonal holiday demand. Additionally, new product and service introductions can significantly impact net sales, cost of sales and operating expenses. The timing of product introductions can also impact the Company's net sales to its indirect distribution channels as these channels are filled with new inventory following a product launch, and channel inventory of an older product often declines as the launch of a newer product approaches. Net sales can also be affected when consumers and distributors anticipate a product introduction.
Human Capital
The Company believes that its people play an important role in its success, and strives to attract, develop and retain the best talent. The Company works to create an inclusive, safe and supportive environment for all of its team members, so that its people can do the best work of their lives. As of September 28, 2024, the Company had approximately 164,000 full-time equivalent employees.
Compensation and Benefits
The Company believes that compensation should be competitive and equitable, and should enable employees to share in the Company's success. The Company recognizes its people are most likely to thrive when they have the resources to meet their needs and the time and support to succeed in their professional and personal lives. In support of this, the Company offers a wide variety of benefits for employees around the world, including health, wellness and time away.
Growth and Development
The Company invests in resources to help its people develop and achieve their career goals. The Company offers programs through Apple University on leadership, management and influence, as well as Apple culture and values. Team members can also take advantage of online classes for business, technical and personal development, as well as learning opportunities to support their well-being.
Workplace Practices and Policies
The Company is an equal opportunity employer committed to inclusion and diversity and to providing a workplace free of harassment or discrimination.
Inclusion and Diversity
The Company is committed to its vision to build and sustain a more inclusive workforce that is representative of the communities it serves. The Company continues to work to increase diverse representation at every level, foster an inclusive culture, and support equitable pay and access to opportunity for all employees.
Engagement
The Company believes that open and honest communication among team members, managers and leaders helps create an open, collaborative work environment where everyone can contribute, grow and succeed. Team members are encouraged to come to their managers with questions, feedback or concerns, and the Company conducts surveys that gauge employee sentiment in areas like career development, manager performance and inclusivity.
Health and Safety
The Company is committed to protecting its team members everywhere it operates. The Company identifies potential workplace risks in order to develop measures to mitigate possible hazards. The Company supports employees with general safety, security and crisis management training, and by putting specific programs in place for those working in potentially high-hazard environments. Additionally, the Company works to protect the safety and security of its team members, visitors and customers through its global security team.
Apple Inc. | 2024 Form 10-K | 4
Available Information
The Company's Annual Reports on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, Current Reports on Form 8-K, and amendments to reports filed pursuant to Sections 13(a) and 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), are filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC"). Such reports and other information filed by the Company with the SEC are available free of charge at investor.apple.com/investor-relations/sec-filings/default.aspx when such reports are available on the SEC's website. The Company periodically provides certain information for investors on its corporate website, www.apple.com, and its investor relations website, investor.apple.com. This includes press releases and other information about financial performance, information on environmental, social and governance matters, and details related to the Company's annual meeting of shareholders. The information contained on the websites referenced in this Form 10-K is not incorporated by reference into this filing. Further, the Company's references to website URLs are intended to be inactive textual references only.
AAPL.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Equity Report: AAPL**  
*Evidence point-in-time: 2025-10-01 (all data prior)*

---

### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory  
- **Revenue**: Q3 2025 (Jun 28) revenue = 94.04B, QoQ -1.4% (from 95.36B in Q2), YoY +9.6% (from 85.78B in Q3 2024). TTM revenue = 408.62B.  
- **Earnings**: Q3 2025 EPS diluted = 1.57, QoQ -4.8% (from 1.65 in Q2), YoY +12.1% (from 1.40 in Q3 2024). TTM net income = 99.28B.  
- **Margins**: Gross margin = 46.7% (latest valuation ratio); operating income Q3 2025 = 28.20B, QoQ -4.7% (from 29.59B in Q2). Net margin = 24.3%.  
- **Cash Flow**: No cash flow data supplied for any period.  
- **Balance Sheet**: Total assets = 331.50B, total equity = 65.83B, total debt = 91.80B. Debt-to-equity = 1.395. Equity has fluctuated: from 56.95B (Q4 2024) to 74.19B (Q2 2024). Total liabilities = 265.67B.

### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience  
- **Earnings Quality**: Net margin stable at ~24.3% across the last four quarters. ROE = 150.8%, indicating high leverage. Q1 2024 (Dec 28) shows a seasonal spike: EPS = 2.40 vs. Q4 2024 (Sep 28) EPS = 0.97, suggesting variability tied to product cycles.  
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Debt-to-equity = 1.395, with debt declining from 106.00B (Q1 2023) to 91.80B (Q3 2025). However, equity is low relative to liabilities (total equity = 65.83B vs. total liabilities = 265.67B). Leverage remains elevated.

### 3. Valuation Versus Supplied History  
- **Current Ratios** (2025-09-30): PE diluted = 38.34, P/B = 57.82, P/S = 9.32, earnings yield = 2.6%.  
- **Historical Context** (over ~1-year window):  
  - PE diluted range: 27.2 to 42.6; current 38.34 is in the upper half but below the peak.  
  - P/B range: 39.1 to 70.1; current 57.82 is above the median.  
  - Net margin has held near 0.243 for most of the period; ROE range 1.4–1.6 (current 1.508).  
- Valuation is elevated relative to historical midpoints but not at extremes.

### 4. Price Level and Return Context Versus Fundamentals  
- **Price** (2025-09-30): 254.63. 52-week high = 260.10, low = 169.21.  
- **Returns**: Quarterly return (last ~63 trading days) = +22.5%; annual return (last ~252 trading days) = +11.9%.  
- **Path**: Price has been volatile—Q1 2025 saw a -14.2% decline, followed by a +22.5% rebound in Q3 2025.  
- **Link to Fundamentals**: YoY revenue +9.6% and EPS +12.1% support the annual price gain, but the sharp quarterly rally (+22.5%) exceeds recent quarterly earnings growth (QoQ EPS -4.8%). Price is near its 52-week high despite flat-to-down sequential earnings.

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside  
- **Upside**: Consistent YoY earnings growth (EPS +12.1%) and stable margins (net margin 24.3%). Debt reduction trend (from 106.00B to 91.80B over 8 quarters). If valuation re-rates toward the upper end of its historical PE range (42.6), price could rise further from current 38.34x.  
- **Downside**: High valuation multiples (PE 38.34, P/B 57.82) relative to history. Lack of cash-flow data hampers assessment of true cash generation. Leverage remains high (debt/equity 1.395). A reversion to the lower PE range (27.2) implies significant downside risk.

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion  
- **Fundamentals**: Solid revenue and earnings growth with stable margins, but balance-sheet leverage is notable and cash-flow evidence is absent.  
- **Valuation**: Stretched relative to historical midpoints but not at peaks.  
- **Price Action**: Recent momentum (+22.5% quarterly) outpaces recent earnings trends, potentially pricing in future growth.  
- **Risk/Reward**: Asymmetric to the downside given high multiples and lack of cash-flow validation. Upside requires sustained growth and valuation expansion; downside is amplified by multiple compression if growth slows. Quantitative stance is **cautious** based on current evidence.
AAPL.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Apple Inc. (AAPL)**  
**Decision Date: 2025-10-01**  

---

### 1. Business / Franchise Read
From the 2024 10-K (filing_date: 2024-11-01), Apple designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables/accessories, and sells related services. Its product lines include iPhone, Mac, iPad, and Wearables/Home/Accessories (e.g., Apple Watch, AirPods, Apple Vision Pro). Services span advertising, AppleCare, cloud, digital content (App Store, subscriptions like Apple Music, TV+, Arcade), and payment services (Apple Pay, Apple Card).  

The company operates through geographic segments: Americas, Europe, Greater China, Japan, and Rest of Asia Pacific. Sales are split 38% direct and 62% indirect (2024). Competition is characterized by aggressive pricing, rapid innovation, and evolving industry standards. Apple emphasizes ecosystem integration, intellectual property ownership, and a strong third-party software ecosystem as competitive advantages. Component supply relies sometimes on single or limited sources, and the business experiences seasonality with higher sales in the first fiscal quarter.

---

### 2. Recent Developments (Supplied Text Dated 2025-08-12 to 2025-09-30)
- **iPhone 17 & AI Catalysts (2025-09-30)**: News indicates iPhone 17 sales and emerging AI features are driving optimism for holiday gains. A web source (2025-09-09) states the stock run was initiated by exceptional Q1 earnings driven by record iPhone 17 sales with advanced AI functionalities.  
- **Automotive Expansion (CarPlay Ultra) (2025-09-30)**: Ford CEO expressed reservations about CarPlay Ultra’s execution, questioning brand integration and control over vehicle functions, though Ford remains committed to Apple.  
- **AI Strategy (Apple Intelligence) (2025-09-11, 2025-09-04, 2025-09-07)**: Web context highlights “Apple Intelligence” as a growth catalyst, with potential to shift Apple from hardware dependence toward ecosystem-wide AI monetization. Siri’s AI revamp could reignite growth beyond current 4–7% revenue forecasts. Analysts expect revenue and EPS CAGR of 6% and 13% from fiscal 2024–2027.  
- **Supply Chain Investment (2025-09-30)**: Apple made a significant investment in MP Materials, a rare earth metals company, indicating strategic moves to secure critical materials amid geopolitical tensions.  
- **Regulatory/Legal Pressures (2025-08-12 to 2025-08-28)**: Apple faces antitrust pressures under EU DMA rules (requiring external payment access and sideloading) and UK competition regulations targeting interoperability. A legal threat from Elon Musk’s xAI alleges anticompetitive favoritism toward OpenAI in App Store rankings (2025-08-13).  
- **Market Context (2025-09-30)**: Broader market cautious amid government shutdown risks and jobs data; technology sector earnings show strong Q3 2025 expectations with AI-driven investment.

---

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**  
- **Regulatory & Legal**: Antitrust enforcement in EU/UK (2025-08-12, 2025-08-28) and potential legal action (e.g., Musk’s threat on 2025-08-13) could force business model changes and increase compliance costs.  
- **Automotive Execution**: CarPlay Ultra faces skepticism from key partners like Ford (2025-09-30), potentially limiting adoption and ecosystem expansion.  
- **Competition & Innovation Pressure**: 10-K notes aggressive pricing, short product cycles, and rivals’ ability to imitate features. AI/Siri rollout must succeed to exceed modest revenue forecasts (2025-09-04).  
- **Supply Chain Constraints**: Reliance on custom/solo-source components and industry shortages (10-K) poses operational risks.

**Offsets:**  
- **Ecosystem Lock-in & Services Growth**: Strong ecosystem integration and recurring revenue from services (e.g., subscriptions, payments) provide stability (10-K).  
- **AI as Growth Catalyst**: Potential monetization of on-device AI (“Apple Intelligence”) could drive hardware upgrades and services revenue (2025-09-11, 2025-09-04).  
- **Brand Loyalty & Direct Distribution**: Strong consumer brand and 38% direct sales channel (10-K) support pricing power and customer retention.  
- **Strategic Investments**: Moves like the MP Materials deal (2025-09-30) mitigate supply chain risks.

---

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Positive catalysts include:** iPhone 17 momentum, AI integration (Apple Intelligence) as a potential growth lever, and strategic supply chain investments. **Negative pressures include:** regulatory/legal headwinds, skepticism around automotive execution, and competitive intensity. The balance of near-term momentum and structural risks warrants a **neutral** qualitative stance pending evidence of AI monetization success and regulatory clarity.  

*Note: All analysis uses only point-in-time evidence provided; no forward multiples or financial projections are inferred.*

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Gross margin = 46.7% (latest valuation ratio)' and 'Net margin = 24.3%' as if these are derived from the latest quarter's income statement, but the raw evidence shows gross_margin=0.467 and net_margin=0.243 as valuation-ratio snapshots (2025-09-30), not computed from Q3 2025 income-statement line items. Q3 2025 gross margin from the income statement is 43.72B/94.04B = 46.5%, which is close but not identical; net margin is 23.43B/94.04B = 24.9%, which differs from the 24.3% cited. The report should clarify whether margins are point-in-time ratio snapshots or computed from the latest quarter.

The raw data labels these as 2024-12-28 (Q1 FY2025) and 2024-09-28 (Q4 FY2024). The report's label 'Q1 2024' is misleading—it is actually Q1 FY2025 (fiscal year ending Sep 2025). This is a minor labeling error but could confuse readers about which fiscal year the spike belongs to.
corrections.required_action: Correct the fiscal-year labels to 'Q1 FY2025 (Dec 2024)' and 'Q4 FY2024 (Sep 2024)' for clarity.

This figure comes from a single web-context source (fool.com, 2025-09-07) and is presented as consensus. The raw evidence does not provide corroboration from multiple analyst sources, so the confidence level of this claim is unclear. The report should note the single-source nature of this estimate.
corrections.required_action: Add a qualifier noting this CAGR estimate is from a single web source and not independently verified across multiple analyst reports.

: The qual report's 'analysts expect revenue and EPS CAGR of 6% and 13%' is sourced from a single Motley Fool article (2025-09-07) and presented without noting it is a single-source estimate, not a verified consensus.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Apple's fundamentals remain solid with YoY revenue growth of +9.6% and EPS growth of +12.1%, but the stock's sharp +22.5% quarterly rally has pushed valuation to the upper half of its historical range (P/E 38.34 vs. 1-year range of 27.2–42.6), leaving limited margin of safety. Regulatory/legal headwinds and the absence of proven AI monetization create meaningful uncertainty that caps upside, while strong ecosystem lock-in and stable margins provide a floor.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Apple's fundamentals remain solid with YoY revenue growth of +9.6% and EPS growth of +12.1%, but the stock's sharp +22.5% quarterly rally has pushed valuation to the upper half of its historical range (P/E 38.34 vs. 1-year range of 27.2–42.6), leaving limited margin of safety. Regulatory/legal headwinds and the absence of proven AI monetization create meaningful uncertainty that caps upside, while strong ecosystem lock-in and stable margins provide a floor.
key_factors: P/E of 38.34 sits in the upper half of its 1-year range (27.2–42.6), with the stock near its 52-week high of 260.10 at 254.63, leaving limited valuation cushion for any earnings disappointment.

YoY earnings growth of +12.1% (Q3 2025 EPS 1.57 vs. 1.40 prior year) and stable net margins (~24.3–24.9%) support the fundamental story, but QoQ EPS declined -4.8% and the +22.5% quarterly price rally materially outpaces recent earnings momentum.

Regulatory and legal headwinds persist: EU DMA enforcement requiring external payment access and sideloading (2025-08-12), UK competition regulations (2025-08-28), and a legal threat from xAI alleging anticompetitive App Store practices (2025-08-13) create ongoing compliance costs and business model risk.

AI monetization remains unproven: while 'Apple Intelligence' and iPhone 17 AI features drive near-term optimism (2025-09-30), analyst revenue CAGR expectations of only 6% through 2027 (single-source estimate, 2025-09-07) suggest the market may be pricing in more than the fundamentals currently support.

HON.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: HON (Honeywell) as of 2025-10-01**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
- **Revenue**: Latest Q2 2025 revenue = $9.32B, up 4.4% QoQ but down 2.7% YoY. TTM revenue = $37.55B.
- **Earnings**: Latest Q2 2025 net income = $1.57B, EPS diluted = $2.45. QoQ EPS growth = +10.4%, YoY EPS growth = +3.8%. TTM net income = $6.01B.
- **Margins**: Gross margin = 37.6%, operating margin = 20.1%, net margin = 15.1% (valuation ratios, likely TTM). Q2 2025 gross margin = 39.3% ($3.66B/$9.32B), operating margin = 25.4% ($2.37B/$9.32B), net margin = 16.8% ($1.57B/$9.32B).
- **Cash-Flow**: No data provided for any period.
- **Balance Sheet**: Total assets = $78.42B, total equity = $16.65B, total debt = $30.24B (Q2 2025). Debt increased from $18.35B (Q3 2023) to $30.24B (Q2 2025). Equity stable around $16.4B–$18.0B over the period.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality**: Net margin stable at ~15–16% over the past year. YoY EPS growth (+3.8%) occurred despite YoY revenue decline (-2.7%), suggesting cost management. Cash-flow data unavailable to assess accruals or cash conversion.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Debt-to-equity ratio = 1.541. Total debt increased by 64.7% from Q3 2023 ($18.35B) to Q2 2025 ($30.24B), while equity grew only 6.2% (from $15.68B to $16.65B). Leverage has risen significantly.

**3. Valuation Versus Company's Own Supplied History**
- Current PE diluted = 22.775, within the 1-year range of 19.8–26.0, near the lower end.
- Current P/B = 7.22, within the 1-year range of 6.3–8.2, also near the lower end.
- Earnings yield = 4.4%.
- Valuation multiples have compressed from recent highs (e.g., PE peaked at 26.0 in the window).

**4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context Versus Fundamental Path**
- **Price**: Latest close = $198.40 (2025-09-30). 52-week range = $169.05–$228.81.
- **Returns**: Quarterly return (last ~63 trading days) = -11.8%. Annual return (last ~252 trading days) = +1.7%.
- **Context**: The recent price decline (-11.8%) contrasts with stable earnings and margins. The stock's valuation has compressed (PE near lower end) despite positive EPS growth.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
- **Upside**: Valuation reversion to the higher end of the historical PE range (26.0) implies a price of ~$226.5 (14% upside from $198.40). Strong ROE (31.7%) and stable margins support earnings durability.
- **Downside**: Elevated debt (debt-to-equity = 1.541) and YoY revenue decline (-2.7%) could pressure earnings. If PE contracts to the lower end (19.8), price would be ~$172.5 (13% downside). Lack of cash-flow data limits quality assessment.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
- The stock trades at a historically low valuation (PE 22.775 vs. 1-year range 19.8–26.0) with stable margins and positive EPS growth. However, balance-sheet leverage has increased materially (debt up 64.7% over two years), and revenue growth is flat to negative. Cash-flow data is unavailable, limiting earnings quality assessment. Risk/reward appears balanced: potential upside from valuation reversion is offset by downside from leverage and revenue headwinds.
HON.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment: Honeywell (HON)**
**Decision Date: 2025-10-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Based on the supplied narrative, Honeywell is a diversified industrial conglomerate with a strong presence in automation, aerospace, and advanced materials. The company is recognized as a leader in smart warehousing and building automation solutions, leveraging robotics and AI. It is actively transforming its portfolio, with a stated strategy to spin off into three independent, focused companies (e.g., the specialty materials business "Solstice") to enhance agility and unlock shareholder value. Its joint venture, Quantinuum, is a noted player in the quantum computing sector.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Corporate Action / Capital Allocation (2025-09-26):** Honeywell announced a dividend increase from $4.52 to $4.76 per share, effective Q4 2025, marking its 16th consecutive annual increase. This was framed as a commitment to shareholder value during its transformation.
*   **Strategic Transformation (2025-08-22, 2025-08-21):** Web context indicates ongoing portfolio rationalization, including the evaluation of its Productivity Solutions and Services (PSS) and Warehouse and Workflow Solutions (WWS) businesses, and the planned spin-off of its Solstice specialty materials unit.
*   **Partnerships & Growth Catalysts (2025-09-15, 2025-09-09):** Honeywell and Redwire are collaborating on a quantum-secure satellite payload for the European Space Agency (target: mid-2026). Its Quantinuum joint venture is receiving significant investment (from Nvidia-backed entities), intensifying competition in quantum computing.
*   **Market Positioning (2025-09-01, 2025-09-24, 2025-09-22):** External reports identify Honeywell as a leader in the growing smart warehousing market. The company's end markets, including pick-to-light systems and remote home monitoring, are projected to see significant growth through 2030-2032.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Litigation & Product Liability (2025-09-18):** Families of victims from the Air India Flight 171 crash have filed a lawsuit against Honeywell and Boeing, alleging a defective fuel cutoff switch (manufactured by Honeywell) was a cause. This presents reputational and potential financial liability risk.
*   **Risk: Regulatory & Operational (2025-09-08):** A report indicates Honeywell paid a fine related to endangering workers, pointing to potential operational and regulatory compliance risks.
*   **Offset: Strategic Simplification (2025-08-22, 2025-09-07):** The planned spin-offs are viewed by some analysts as a catalyst to unlock value by creating more focused, agile entities that can better compete in specialized markets.
*   **Offset: Shareholder Return Commitment (2025-09-26):** The consistent, long-term dividend growth signals financial discipline and a commitment to returning capital, which can support investor confidence during transformation.

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Cautiously Positive.** The narrative is dominated by Honeywell's active strategic transformation into focused pure-play companies, which is a potential value-unlocking catalyst. This is supported by a consistent track record of dividend growth and leadership positions in key growth markets like automation and quantum technology. However, the assessment is tempered by material, near-term litigation risk stemming from a major aerospace accident and a reported regulatory fine, which introduce uncertainty and potential liability. The balance of evidence suggests a company in strategic motion with clear growth vectors, but not without significant, specific overhangs.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Debt increased from $18.35B (Q3 2023) to $30.24B (Q2 2025)' and '64.7% over two years.' The raw balance sheet shows total_debt=18.35B at 2023-09-30 and 30.24B at 2025-06-30, which is ~64.7% over ~21 months (not two years). The percentage is arithmetically correct but the 'two years' framing slightly overstates the time period. Minor — no directional impact.
corrections.required_action: No action required; noted for precision only.

corrections.issue: Quant report claims 'ROE (31.7%)' supports earnings durability. The raw valuation ratios show roe=0.317, which is consistent. However, the report does not note that ROE is elevated partly by the high leverage (D/E 1.541) and that equity actually declined from 18.02B (Q1 2025) to 16.65B (Q2 2025) — a ~7.6% QoQ drop — which mechanically inflates ROE. This is a material omission for earnings quality assessment.
corrections.required_action: PM should note that the 31.7% ROE is partly leverage-driven and that equity declined QoQ, which inflates the ratio. Cash-flow data remains unavailable, so true earnings quality cannot be confirmed.

corrections.issue: Qual report states 'Honeywell paid a fine related to endangering workers' without quantifying it. The raw risk evidence specifies a '$20k fine.' The omission of the amount understates the materiality assessment — $20k is immaterial for a company of Honeywell's scale. The qual report's framing of this as a 'material qualitative risk' is arguably overstated given the trivial fine amount.
corrections.required_action: PM should note the $20k fine is immaterial to Honeywell's financials; the litigation risk (Air India crash lawsuit) is the more significant overhang.

unsupported_claims: : The qual report describes the worker-safety fine as a 'material qualitative risk' — the raw evidence shows a $20k fine, which is immaterial for a $37.5B revenue company. The characterization overstates the financial significance of this event.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Honeywell trades near the lower end of its 1-year valuation range (PE 22.8x vs. 19.8–26.0 range) after an 11.8% quarterly decline, with stable margins and positive EPS growth (+3.8% YoY) offset by materially elevated leverage (D/E 1.54, debt up 65% over 21 months) and flat-to-negative revenue (-2.7% YoY). The strategic transformation into three focused companies is a meaningful catalyst, but the absence of cash-flow data and the leverage trajectory limit conviction.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Honeywell trades near the lower end of its 1-year valuation range (PE 22.8x vs. 19.8–26.0 range) after an 11.8% quarterly decline, with stable margins and positive EPS growth (+3.8% YoY) offset by materially elevated leverage (D/E 1.54, debt up 65% over 21 months) and flat-to-negative revenue (-2.7% YoY). The strategic transformation into three focused companies is a meaningful catalyst, but the absence of cash-flow data and the leverage trajectory limit conviction.
key_factors: Valuation near lower end of 1-year range: PE 22.8x (range 19.8–26.0) and P/B 7.22x (range 6.3–8.2) after -11.8% quarterly price decline to $198.40, offering ~14% upside to historical PE ceiling of 26.0x.

Balance-sheet leverage materially elevated: debt rose from $18.35B (Q3 2023) to $30.24B (Q2 2025), a 65% increase over 21 months, with D/E at 1.541; ROE of 31.7% is partly mechanically inflated by this leverage and a 7.6% QoQ equity decline.
Earnings resilience despite revenue headwinds: Q2 2025 EPS $2.45 (+3.8% YoY, +10.4% QoQ) with operating margin of 25.4%, demonstrating cost discipline as revenue declined 2.7% YoY to $9.32B.

Strategic transformation catalyst: planned spin-off into three companies (including Solstice specialty materials) plus dividend increase to $4.76/share (16th consecutive year) signal value-unlocking potential, though Air India Flight 171 lawsuit introduces reputational and financial liability risk.

Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2025-08-04 section=business period_end=2025-06-30
Item 1. Business.
The Procter & Gamble Company (the Company) is a world-leading multinational consumer goods company focused on providing trusted, branded products of superior quality, performance and value to improve the lives of consumers around the world - now and for generations to come. Our products are sold in about 180 countries and territories throughout the world. The Company was incorporated in Ohio in 1905, having first been established as a New Jersey corporation in 1890, and was built from a business founded in Cincinnati in 1837 by William Procter and James Gamble.
Additional information required by this item is incorporated herein by reference to Management's Discussion and Analysis (MD&A); and Notes 1 and 2 to our Consolidated Fina
…[truncated 5392 chars]…
 do business. In the United States, many of our products and manufacturing operations are subject to one or more federal or state regulatory agencies, including the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC). We are also subject to anti-corruption laws and regulations, such as the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, and antitrust and competition laws and regulations that govern our dealings with suppliers, customers, competitors and government officials.
In addition, many foreign jurisdictions in which we do business have regulations and regulatory bodies that govern similar aspects of our operations and products, in some cases to an even more significant degree. We are also subject to expanding laws and regulations related to environmental protection and other sustainability-related matters, non-financial reporting and diligence, labor and employment, trade, taxation and privacy and data protection, including the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation and similar regulations in states within the United States and in countries around the world.
The Company has in place compliance programs and internal and external experts to help guide our business in complying with these and other existing laws and regulations that apply to us around the globe; and we have made, and plan to continue making, necessary expenditures for compliance with these laws and regulations. We also expect that our many suppliers, consultants and other third parties working on our behalf share our commitment to compliance, and we have policies and procedures in place to manage these relationships, though they inherently involve a lesser degree of control over operations and governance. We do not expect that the Company's expenditures for compliance with current government regulations, including current environmental regulations, will have a material effect on our total capital expenditures, earnings or competitive position in fiscal year 2026 as compared to prior periods.
Human Capital
. Our employees are a key source of competitive advantage. Their actions, guided by our Purpose, Values and Principles (PVPs), are critical to the long-term success of our business. We aim to retain our talented employees by offering competitive compensation and benefits, strong career development and a respectful and inclusive culture that provides equal opportunity for all.
Our Board of Directors, through the Compensation and Leadership Development Committee (C&LD Committee), provides oversight of the Company's policies and strategy relating to talent, including equality and inclusion, as well as the Company's compensation principles and practices. The C&LD Committee also evaluates and approves the Company's compensation plans, policies and programs applicable to our senior executives.
Employees
As of June 30, 2025, the Company had approximately 109,000 employees, an increase of 2% versus the prior year due to business growth. The total number of employees is an estimate of total Company employees excluding interns, co-ops, contractors and employees of joint ventures. 49% of our employees are in manufacturing roles and 28% of our employees are located in the United States. 42% of our global employees are women and 32% of our U.S. employees identify as multicultural.
Training and Development
We focus on attracting, developing and retaining the broadest pool of talent available, both from universities and the broader market. We recruit from universities across markets in which we compete and are generally able to select from the top talent. We focus on developing our employees by providing a variety of job experiences, training programs and skill development opportunities. Given our develop-from-within model for staffing most of our senior leadership positions, it is particularly important for us to ensure holistic growth and full engagement of our employees.
Equality and Inclusion
As a global consumer products company, P&G serves consumers around the world with operations in approximately 70 countries. We believe that it is good for business that our workforce is drawn from the best available talent from communities worldwide, with insights about, connectivity to and understanding of all our consumers. Further, a foundational component of P&G's strategy and success has been to foster an inclusive work environment, in which we develop and advance the very best capabilities that all our people have to offer. Globally, we aspire to reflect the diversity of the consumers we serve in the communities where we operate and are committed to creating a superior employee experience for all. Our aspiration is founded on our longstanding values and principles for equal opportunity and compliance with the law.
Compensation and Benefits
Market-competitive compensation and reward programs are critical elements of our employee value equation to attract and retain the best talent. Our total rewards programs are based on the principles of paying for performance, paying competitively versus peer companies that we compete with for talent in the marketplace and focusing on long-term success through a combination of short-term and long-term incentive programs. We also offer competitive benefit programs, including retirement plans and health insurance, in line with local country practices, with flexibility to accommodate the needs of a diverse workforce.
Sustainability
. Environmental sustainability is integrated into our business strategy. We are focused on designing and manufacturing irresistibly superior products that are more sustainable. We aim to reduce our own environmental footprint and
The Procter & Gamble Company        3
enable our consumers to reduce their footprint without compromising on the performance of the products they use. We develop and license technologies that can be used across industries to improve environmental sustainability at a broader scale.
Combined, this approach intends to positively impact the total environmental impact of the Company while driving market growth and value creation.
In 2021, the Company announced a 2040 net zero ambition. Our Climate Transition Action Plan outlines the Company's ongoing efforts toward reducing greenhouse gas emissions across scopes 1 and 2 and elements of scope 3. The Company has also declared ambitions towards purchasing renewable electricity for our operations, reducing intensity of virgin petroleum-based plastic in packaging, designing more consumer packaging to be recyclable or reusable, responsible sourcing of key forest-based commodities, improving efficiency of water usage in our operations and driving a global portfolio of water restoration projects in key water basins. While we have met or exceeded some of the goals and are making significant progress against others, scalable infrastructure and cost-effective solutions are not yet available to us to fully deliver against some of our stated ambitions.
Sustainability related disclosures included in this Annual Report, our Proxy Statement and our sustainability reports are informed by standards and guidelines such as the Global Reporting Initiative (GRI) and the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures (TCFD). The "materiality" thresholds in those standards and guidelines may differ from the concept of "materiality" for purposes of the federal securities laws and disclosures required by the Commission's rules in this Annual Report. References to our sustainability reports and website are for informational purposes only and neither the sustainability reports nor the other information on our website is incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K. Additional detailed information on our sustainability efforts can be found on our website at https://pginvestor.com/esg.
PG.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Analysis: Procter & Gamble (PG) as of 2025-10-01**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
- **Revenue**: Latest Q3 (2025-03-31) revenue is $19.78B, representing a QoQ decline of 9.6% from $21.88B and a YoY decline of 2.1% from $20.20B. TTM revenue (last 4 quarters) is $83.93B.
- **Earnings**: Q3 diluted EPS is $1.54, down 18.1% QoQ but up 1.3% YoY. TTM net income is $15.58B.
- **Margins**: Q3 gross margin is 51.0% ($10.08B / $19.78B). Q3 operating margin is 23.1% ($4.56B / $19.78B). Q3 net margin is 19.2% ($3.79B / $19.78B). The valuation data shows net margin at 0.186 and gross margin at 0.513 as of 2025-09-30.
- **Cash-Flow**: Unavailable for all periods.
- **Balance-Sheet**: Total equity grew from $48.01B (2023-09-30) to $52.55B (2025-03-31). Total assets are $122.98B with total liabilities of $70.44B, yielding a debt-to-equity ratio of 1.34.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality**: YoY EPS growth (+1.3%) is positive despite a sharp QoQ revenue decline. The TTM operating income is $19.98B. Without cash flow data, cash conversion cannot be assessed. Gross margin remains above 50%.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Equity has increased steadily over the last seven quarters. The leverage ratio (total liabilities/equity) is 1.34 as of 2025-03-31. Asset base ($122.98B) is substantial relative to equity ($52.55B).

**3. Valuation versus Supplied History**
- Current P/E (diluted) is 24.16 (as of 2025-09-30), within the one-year range of 23.7 to 30.8.
- Current P/B is 7.16, within the one-year range of 7.0 to 8.5.
- The P/E has compressed from 28.74 (2024-10-01) to 24.16, a 16.0% decline over the period.
- Earnings yield is 4.1% (1/24.16).

**4. Price Level and Return Context**
- Price as of 2025-09-30 is $153.65, down 11.3% annually (from $173.21) and down 4.7% quarterly (from $161.22).
- The 52-week high is $180.43 and low is $149.91.
- Price has declined for four consecutive quarters, with the steepest recent drop of 5.4% in Q1 2025.
- The annual price decline (-11.3%) contrasts with a positive YoY EPS change (+1.3%).

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
- **Upside**: P/E is near the bottom of its one-year range (23.7-30.8). Equity and margins have remained stable. If revenue stabilizes, multiple expansion could occur.
- **Downside**: Revenue has declined for two consecutive quarters (QoQ -9.6%, YoY -2.1%). The most recent quarter's EPS fell 18.1% QoQ. Continued revenue weakness could pressure margins and earnings further.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The stock trades at a historically low valuation (P/E 24.16 vs. one-year high of 30.8) despite stable margins and growing equity. However, the fundamental trajectory shows decelerating revenue and a sharp recent quarterly earnings drop. The annual price decline (-11.3%) has outpaced the modest YoY EPS gain (+1.3%), suggesting negative sentiment. Risk remains in the near-term revenue trend; reward is contingent on a fundamental reversal to justify multiple expansion. Quantitative evidence points to a balanced risk/reward profile pending revenue stabilization.
PG.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Assessment: Procter & Gamble (PG)  
**Date of Analysis:** 2025-10-01  
**Evidence Window:** 2025-08-04 to 2025-09-29

---

#### 1. Business/Franchise Read
P&G is a world-leading multinational consumer goods company (10-K, 2025-08-04) with a diversified portfolio of daily-use branded products sold in about 180 countries. Its business model focuses on balanced top- and bottom-line growth by driving "irresistible superiority" across product performance, packaging, brand communication, retail execution, and value. The company leverages R&D, consumer insights, and marketing to innovate across key product categories, utilizing multi-channel distribution including e-commerce and direct-to-consumer. It operates in highly competitive markets against both global branded and private-label competitors, often holding leadership positions. A key customer dependency exists, with Walmart accounting for 16% of total sales (10-K, 2025-08-04).

#### 2. Recent Narrative Developments (Last 50 Days)
*   **Product & Advertising:** The National Advertising Division reviewed Crest toothpaste claims, finding most supported but recommending modification of the "extra strength fluoride" claim (2025-08-19).
*   **Growth & Outlook:** Analyst narratives cite P&G's "operational excellence" and consistent dividend growth (67 consecutive years mentioned) (2025-09-26). The company's fiscal 2026 outlook suggests a continued focus on its integrated growth strategy (2025-09-22).
*   **Financial Performance Context:** A recent analysis noted PG stock underperformed the S&P 500 over a five-year period (2025-08-29). Commentary following Q3 2025 indicated a sales miss, with potential future scenarios hinging on restructuring and market share gains (2025-09-19).
*   **Corporate Governance & ESG:** An NRDC article (2025-08-22) accused P&G of removing mentions of prohibiting forest degradation from its supply chain policy after shareholder pressure.
*   **Regulatory/Legal:** A GST Authority in India ordered P&G to deposit ₹6.88 Lakh in a profiteering case (2025-09-12).

#### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
*   **Risk: Legal and Regulatory Exposure.** The company faces a class action lawsuit alleging it failed to disclose unsafe lead levels in Tampax tampons (2025-08-25, 2025-08-19). Separate regulatory actions include the Indian GST profiteering order (2025-09-12) and scrutiny over advertising claims (2025-08-19). The 10-K acknowledges being subject to wide-ranging global regulations (10-K, 2025-08-04).
    *   **Offset:** The company states it has compliance programs and internal/external experts, and does not expect compliance expenditures to materially impact earnings (10-K, 2025-08-04).
*   **Risk: ESG & Reputational Concerns.** Allegations of "doubling down on forest degradation" could impact brand perception and draw activist attention (2025-08-22).
    *   **Offset:** The 10-K outlines an integrated sustainability strategy with a 2040 net zero ambition and specific environmental goals, positioning it as a core part of the business model (10-K, 2025-08-04).
*   **Risk: Competitive & Market Performance.** The company operates in highly competitive markets (10-K, 2025-08-04) and recent stock performance has underperformed the broader market over a five-year horizon (2025-08-29).
    *   **Offset:** The franchise is frequently characterized as "recession-proof" and a "Dividend King" in supportive narratives, emphasizing its stability and consistent shareholder returns (2025-09-29, 2025-08-31).

#### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral, with stable franchise characteristics offset by near-term legal and reputational headwinds.**

The evidence confirms P&G's enduring, globally scaled consumer staples franchise with a clear strategy focused on product superiority and operational execution. Its recognition as a Dividend King underscores its perceived defensive qualities. However, the recent narrative is tempered by active legal challenges concerning product safety and ESG practices, alongside commentary highlighting recent stock underperformance and a sales miss. The company's outlook points to strategic continuity (2025-09-22), but the near-term risk profile is elevated by specific regulatory and reputational matters disclosed in the supplied text.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Revenue has declined for two consecutive quarters (QoQ -9.6%, YoY -2.1%)' in the downside section. The QoQ -9.6% is for Q3 only; the prior quarter (Q2) showed QoQ revenue growth of +0.6% (from $21.74B to $21.88B). The YoY -2.1% is also Q3-specific. The claim of 'two consecutive quarters' of revenue decline is not supported by the supplied trajectory data, which shows Q2 revenue ($21.88B) was higher than Q1 ($21.74B).
corrections.required_action: Correct the downside bullet to state that Q3 revenue declined QoQ and YoY, rather than claiming two consecutive quarters of decline. The Q2-to-Q3 drop is the only consecutive quarterly decline shown in the data.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Price has declined for four consecutive quarters, with the steepest recent drop of 5.4% in Q1 2025.' The quarterly path data shows: Q4 2024 delta = -1.8%, Q1 2025 delta = +0.2%, Q2 2025 delta = -5.4%, Q3 2025 delta = -4.7%. The Q1 2025 delta is +0.2%, not a decline. Only three of the four quarters show price declines, and the steepest drop (-5.4%) occurred in Q2 2025 (the 2025-03-31 to 2025-07-01 period), not 'Q1 2025.'
corrections.required_action: Correct to state price declined in three of the last four quarters, and clarify the steepest quarterly drop of -5.4% corresponds to the Q2 2025 period (2025-03-31 to 2025-07-01).

corrections.issue: Qual report states 'A recent analysis noted PG stock underperformed the S&P 500 over a five-year period (2025-08-29).' The raw evidence article summary says a $1,000 investment grew to $1,270 vs. S&P 500 growing to $2,015. This is accurate per the pack. However, the qual report also references 'a sales miss' from 2025-09-19, but the raw evidence summary for that article describes 'potential scenarios' including restructuring and market share gains — it does not explicitly confirm a 'sales miss' in the summary text provided. The phrase 'sales miss' may be drawn from the article title ('Sales Miss Overshadows Earnings') but the summary itself does not include this detail.
corrections.required_action: Minor: The 'sales miss' reference is plausible from the article title visible in the web context, so this is acceptable. No correction required, but note the summary text alone does not confirm it.

unsupported_claims: Quant report downside section: 'Revenue has declined for two consecutive quarters (QoQ -9.6%, YoY -2.1%)' — The QoQ -9.6% and YoY -2.1% figures are Q3-specific. The raw trajectory shows Q2 revenue ($21.88B) exceeded Q1 ($21.74B), so only one consecutive quarterly decline (Q2→Q3) is evidenced, not two.

Quant report: 'Price has declined for four consecutive quarters, with the steepest recent drop of 5.4% in Q1 2025' — The quarterly price path shows Q1 2025 (2024-12-26 to 2025-03-31) had a delta of +0.2%, not a decline. The -5.4% drop corresponds to the Q2 2025 period (2025-03-31 to 2025-07-01).
Quant report: 'The annual price decline (-11.3%) contrasts with a positive YoY EPS change (+1.3%)' — Both figures are directly supported by the raw packs and are accurate.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: PG trades at a historically low P/E (24.16x vs. 30.8x one-year high) with stable margins above 50% and growing equity, but near-term revenue deceleration (-2.1% YoY in Q3) and persistent legal/ESG headwinds limit conviction in a meaningful reversion thesis. The risk/reward is balanced, offering modest upside from valuation mean reversion if revenue stabilizes, but lacking a clear catalyst for multiple expansion.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: PG trades at a historically low P/E (24.16x vs. 30.8x one-year high) with stable margins above 50% and growing equity, but near-term revenue deceleration (-2.1% YoY in Q3) and persistent legal/ESG headwinds limit conviction in a meaningful reversion thesis. The risk/reward is balanced, offering modest upside from valuation mean reversion if revenue stabilizes, but lacking a clear catalyst for multiple expansion.
key_factors: P/E of 24.16x is near the bottom of its one-year range (23.7x-30.8x), with P/B of 7.16x also compressed (7.0x-8.5x range), suggesting valuation has already priced in much of the recent fundamental weakness.

Q3 FY25 revenue declined -2.1% YoY to $19.78B and -9.6% QoQ, while EPS grew only +1.3% YoY to $1.54, indicating deceleration from the prior quarter's +34.3% EPS growth and raising questions about top-line momentum.

Gross margin remains above 50% (51.0% in Q3) and total equity grew to $52.55B, demonstrating balance sheet resilience, but the debt-to-equity ratio of 1.34 and absence of cash flow data limit full earnings quality assessment.

Active legal risks include a class action over Tampax lead levels (2025-08-25) and ESG allegations regarding forest degradation policy changes (2025-08-22), adding reputational and regulatory uncertainty to the near-term outlook.

s. If any of our government contracts were to be terminated for default, generally the U.S. government would pay only for the work that has been accepted and could require us to pay the difference between the original contract price and the cost to re-procure the contract items,
3
Table of Contents
net of the work accepted from the original contract. The U.S. government can also hold us liable for damages resulting from the default.
Commercial Aircraft.
In the U.S., our commercial aircraft products are required to comply with FAA regulations governing production and quality systems, airworthiness and installation approvals, repair procedures and continuing operational safety. For example, as a result of the Alaska Airlines 737-9 accident in January 2024, the FAA investigated the 737 quality control system, including Spirit AeroSystems Holdings, Inc. (Spirit), and increased its oversight of our production and quality and safety management systems. In addition, the FAA communicated it will not approve production rate increases beyond 38 per month or additional production lines until Boeing has complied with required quality and safety standards. New aircraft models and new derivative aircraft are required to obtain FAA certification prior to entry into service. Outside the U.S., similar requirements exist for airworthiness, installation and operational approvals. These requirements are generally administered by the national aviation authorities of each country and, in the case of Europe, coordinated by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency.
Environmental.
We are subject to various federal, state, local and non-U.S. laws and regulations relating to environmental protection, including the utilization, discharge, treatment, storage, disposal and remediation of hazardous substances and wastes. We could also be affected by laws and regulations relating to climate change, including laws limiting or otherwise related to greenhouse gas emissions. These laws and regulations could lead to increased environmental compliance expenditures, increased energy and raw materials costs and new and/or additional investment in designs and technologies. We continually assess our compliance status and management of environmental matters to monitor compliance with applicable environmental laws and regulations. Investigation, remediation and operation and maintenance costs associated with environmental compliance and management of sites are a normal, recurring part of our operations. These costs often are allowable costs under our contracts with the U.S. government. It is reasonably possible that costs incurred to ensure continued environmental compliance could have a material impact on our results of operations, financial condition or cash flows if additional work requirements or more stringent clean-up standards are imposed by regulators, new areas of soil, air and groundwater contamination are discovered and/or expansions of work scope are prompted by the results of investigations. For additional information relating to environmental contingencies, see Note 14 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Non-U.S. Sales.
Our non-U.S. sales are subject to both U.S. and non-U.S. governmental regulations and procurement policies and practices, including regulations relating to import-export control, tariffs, investment, exchange controls, anti-corruption and repatriation of earnings. Non-U.S. sales are also subject to varying currency, political and economic risks.
Raw Materials, Parts and Subassemblies
We are highly dependent on the availability and quality of essential materials, parts and subassemblies from our suppliers and subcontractors. The most important raw materials required for our aerospace products are aluminum (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions), titanium (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions) and composites (including carbon and boron). Although alternative sources generally exist for these raw materials, qualification of the sources could take a year or more. Many major components and product equipment items are procured or subcontracted on a sole-source basis. We continue to work with a small number of sole-source suppliers to ensure continuity of supply for certain items.
Suppliers
We are dependent upon the ability of a large number of U.S. and non-U.S. suppliers and subcontractors to meet performance specifications, quality standards and delivery schedules at our anticipated costs. While we maintain an extensive qualification and performance surveillance system to control risk
4
Table of Contents
associated with such reliance on third parties, failure of suppliers or subcontractors to meet commitments has and could continue to adversely affect product quality, production schedules and program/contract profitability, thereby jeopardizing our ability to fulfill commitments to our customers. We have also experienced significant increases in supplier prices which are adversely affecting our business. We are also dependent on the availability of energy sources, such as electricity, at affordable prices.
Seasonality
No material portion of our business is considered to be seasonal.
Executive Officers of the Registrant
See "Item 10. Directors, Executive Officers and Corporate Governance" in Part III.
Other Information
Boeing was originally incorporated in the State of Washington in 1916 and reincorporated in Delaware in 1934. Our principal executive offices are located at 929 Long Bridge Drive, Arlington, Virginia 22202, and our telephone number is (703) 465-3500.

# Boeing (BA) — Quantitative Report
**Decision date: 2025-10-01 | Latest filing: 2025-07-29 (Q2 2025)**

---

## 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow & Balance-Sheet Trajectory

**Revenue** is recovering but volatile. Q2 2025 revenue of $22.75B is the highest in the 8-quarter window, up +34.9% YoY from Q2 2024's $16.87B and +16.7% QoQ from Q1 2025's $19.50B. TTM revenue totals $75.33B. The trajectory is non-linear: revenue peaked at $22.02B in Q4 2023, collapsed to $15.24B by Q4 2024, and has since rebounded.

**Earnings** remain deeply negative across all 8 reported quarters. Q2 2025 EPS is -$0.92 (net income -$612M). The worst quarter was Q3 2024 at EPS -$9.97 (net income -$6.17B). TTM net income is -$10.68B. A brief improvement in Q1 2025 (EPS -$0.16, net income -$31M) reversed in Q2.

**Margins** are negative on a trailing basis. Q2 2025 gross margin is 10.7% ($2.44B / $22.75B), operating margin is -0.8% (-$176M / $22.75B), and net margin is -2.7%. TTM gross profit is -$244M; TTM operating income is -$9.25B. The TTM gross margin is negative, meaning the company has not covered direct costs over the trailing year. Q3 and Q4 2024 were catastrophic, with gross losses of -$3.51B and -$1.59B respectively.

**Cash flow** data is unavailable for all 8 quarters. Free cash flow and operating cash flow cannot be assessed.

**Balance sheet** shows negative equity of -$3.30B as of Q2 2025, though this has improved from -$23.56B in Q3 2024. Total debt stands at $53.14B against total assets of $155.12B (debt-to-assets: 34.3%). Total liabilities of $158.42B exceed total assets, indicating technical insolvency. The last available cash figure is $10.50B from Q3 2024; no recent cash balance is supplied.

---

## 2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality is poor.** Every one of the 8 reported quarters carries negative net income. TTM net income of -$10.68B on $75.33B revenue implies a TTM net margin of approximately -14.2%. The supplied net margin series (12 data points from Oct 2024 to Sep 2025) ranges from -20% to 0%, never turning positive. The YoY EPS comparison worsened -60.5% despite +34.9% revenue growth, indicating that cost structure has not scaled with the revenue recovery. Gross profit swung from +$1.23B in Q2 2024 to -$3.51B in Q3 2024 before recovering to +$2.44B in Q2 2025, suggesting highly lumpy, contract-driven recognition.

**Balance-sheet resilience is critically low.** Negative equity of -$3.30B means the book value per share is negative. Debt of $53.14B is substantial relative to the $155.12B asset base. Without cash-flow data, debt service capacity cannot be directly measured, but the persistent operating losses (TTM operating income: -$9.25B) indicate the company is burning capital. The improvement in equity from -$23.56B to -$3.30B over three quarters likely reflects accounting adjustments rather than organic earnings generation, given continued net losses.

---

## 3. Valuation vs. Company's Own Supplied History

**P/S ratio:** 2.168× on TTM revenue of $75.33B implies a market cap of approximately $163B.

**Earnings-based multiples are not computable.** PE diluted, EV/EBIT, FCF yield, and P/B are all unavailable (n/a) due to negative earnings and negative book equity. The supplied range for net_margin over the 365-day window is -0.20 to 0.00 — never positive.

**The stock cannot be called cheap on fundamentals.** With negative TTM earnings, negative equity, and no positive cash-flow data, there is no earnings or asset-based anchor for valuation. The P/S of 2.168× is the only available metric, and it prices the company at a premium to revenue despite a broken margin structure. The net margin has improved from -17.8% (Feb–Apr 2025) to -14.2% (Aug–Sep 2025), but remains deeply negative.

---

## 4. Price Level & Return Context vs. Fundamental Path

**Current price:** $215.83 (close on 2025-09-30).

**52-week range:** $128.88 – $242.69. The stock sits 11.1% below its 52-week high and 67.5% above its 52-week low.

**Returns:**
- Quarterly (63 trading days from 2025-07-01): **+2.9%** ($209.79 → $215.83)
- Annual (252 trading days from 2024-09-26): **+39.6%** ($154.58 → $215.83)

**Quarterly path detail:**
- Q4 2024 (Sep 26 → Dec 26): +16.7% ($154.58 → $180.38)
- Q1 2025 (Dec 26 → Mar 31): -5.4% ($180.38 → $170.55)
- Q2 2025 (Mar 31 → Jul 1): +23.0% ($170.55 → $209.79)
- Q3 2025 (Jul 1 → Sep 30): +2.9% ($209.79 → $215.83)

**Disconnect with fundamentals:** The stock has returned +39.6% over the past year while TTM net income is -$10.68B and equity is negative. The Q2 2025 rally of +23.0% coincided with a quarter of -$612M net income and worsening EPS (-$0.92 vs. Q1's -$0.16). Price momentum has significantly outpaced the fundamental trajectory, which shows only modest revenue improvement against persistent, deep losses.

---

## 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside

**Upside:**
- Revenue momentum is real: +34.9% YoY in Q2 2025, with QoQ acceleration of +16.7%. If gross margins normalize toward the Q2 2025 level of 10.7% and operating leverage improves, the path to operating breakeven exists. Q1 2025 showed a positive operating income of $461M, demonstrating the business can generate operating profit at ~$19.5B quarterly revenue.
- Equity improved from -$23.56B to -$3.30B over three quarters, a $20.26B improvement, suggesting the balance sheet may be repairable.

**Downside:**
- Negative equity of -$3.30B with $53.14B in debt creates refinancing and dilution risk. If losses persist at the TTM rate of -$10.68B/year, equity erodes further.
- The stock's 52-week low of $128.88 represents -40.3% downside from the current price. A retest is plausible if the revenue recovery stalls or Q3 2025 margins disappoint.
- TTM gross profit is negative (-$244M), meaning the core business has not generated positive gross margin over the trailing year — a fundamental viability concern.
- No cash-flow data means the actual cash burn rate is unknown, representing an unquantifiable risk.

---

## 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

**The risk/reward is unfavorable at current levels.** The stock has priced in a substantial recovery (+39.6% annual return, P/S of 2.168×) while the financials show: (1) TTM net losses of -$10.68B, (2) negative TTM gross profit of -$244M, (3) negative equity of -$3.30B, (4) $53.14B in debt, and (5) no available cash-flow evidence to confirm cash generation. The Q1 2025 operating profit of $461M was a positive signal but reversed in Q2 2025 (operating loss of -$176M). The price has already moved 67.5% off its low, compressing the asymmetry: upside requires sustained margin normalization and earnings inflection that the numbers have not yet confirmed, while downside to the 52-week low of $128.88 is -40.3%. The quantitative evidence does not support a favorable risk/reward at $215.83 given the current earnings trajectory and balance-sheet condition.
BA.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: Boeing (BA)**
**Decision Date: 2025-10-01**

### 1. Business / Franchise Read
Boeing is a global aerospace firm organized into three segments: **Commercial Airplanes (BCA)**, which produces the 737, 767, 777, 787, and 777X; **Defense, Space & Security (BDS)**, serving military and government customers; and **Global Services (BGS)**, providing aftermarket support. The company is a leading commercial aircraft producer with a vast order backlog, particularly concentrated on the 737 MAX family. It operates in highly competitive markets against Airbus, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, and others, and is heavily dependent on a large global supplier network. (Source: 10-K Business Section, 2025-02-03).

### 2. Recent Narrative Developments (PIT: 2025-08-12 to 2025-09-30)
*   **Regulatory Milestone:** The FAA is easing production restrictions and has restored Boeing's partial authority to perform final safety checks on 737 MAX and 787 aircraft, signaling a key step in operational recovery. (2025-09-26)
*   **Operational Recovery:** Reports indicate an acceleration in aircraft deliveries, with Bank of America maintaining a Buy rating and a noted 63% YoY growth in delivery count for Q2 2025. Major orders were secured from Norwegian Group and Turkish Airlines. (2025-09-19, 2025-09-26, 2025-09-12)
*   **Competitive Pressure:** Otto Aerospace unveiled the Phantom 3500, a business jet claiming significantly lower fuel burn, representing a sustainable technology leap in the broader aerospace market. (2025-09-30)
*   **Ongoing Legal & Regulatory Actions:** The FAA has proposed $3.14 million in civil penalties against Boeing for safety violations tied to 737 production. Separately, families have filed a lawsuit regarding the Air India Flight 171 crash, alleging a defective part. (2025-09-18, 2025-09-12, 2025-09-17)
*   **Labor Relations:** The company's major contract with IAM District 751, representing 21% of its workforce, expires in September 2028, following a strike in late 2024 that adversely impacted production. (Source: 10-K Business Section, 2025-02-03)

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Persistent Regulatory & Legal Overhang:** The proposed FAA fines and the ongoing Air India crash lawsuit highlight unresolved safety and quality control issues. These create financial, reputational, and production uncertainty. (2025-09-12, 2025-09-18)
*   **Production Execution:** The FAA has explicitly conditioned further production rate increases on Boeing meeting required quality and safety standards. Failure to execute safely could stall the recovery trajectory. (Source: 10-K Business Section, 2025-02-03; 2025-09-26)
*   **Supplier & Geopolitical Dependencies:** The business is highly reliant on a large supplier network for critical materials and sole-source components, exposing it to supply chain and price volatility risks. Non-U.S. sales are subject to tariffs, sanctions, and political risks. (Source: 10-K Business Section, 2025-02-03; 2025-09-26)
*   **Competitive Dynamics:** The 737 MAX competes aggressively with the Airbus A320neo. New entrants, like Otto Aerospace in business jets, could pressure market share in adjacent segments. (2025-09-30; Source: 10-K Business Section, 2025-02-03)

**Offsets:**
*   **Large Backlog & Market Outlook:** Boeing's 737 MAX backlog ensures future demand visibility. The long-term commercial market outlook projects strong passenger growth driving new aircraft needs. (2025-08-21, 2025-09-02)
*   **Recent Operational Momentum:** The recent surge in deliveries and restoration of some FAA authority provide tangible evidence of improving execution, which is a core catalyst for re-rating the stock. (2025-09-19, 2025-09-26, 2025-09-11)
*   **Diversified Revenue Base:** Defense and Services segments provide counter-cyclicality and aftermarket revenue streams, insulating the business from pure commercial aviation cycles. (Source: 10-K Business Section, 2025-02-03)

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Cautiously Positive.** The qualitative narrative has shifted from acute crisis to one of nascent operational recovery, supported by concrete regulatory and delivery milestones in September 2025. The restored FAA authority is a significant positive catalyst for production normalization. However, this positive momentum is balanced by the weight of unresolved legal liabilities, lingering regulatory scrutiny over safety culture, and the need to consistently execute against a complex production ramp. The franchise remains sound due to its market position and backlog, but the path to sustainable re-rating is contingent on navigating these persistent risks without further material setbacks.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Q1 2025 showed a positive operating income of $461M' as an upside signal, but the raw evidence shows Q1 2025 net income was -$31M (EPS -$0.16). The $461M operating income is confirmed in the income statement, but the report's framing that 'the business can generate operating profit at ~$19.5B quarterly revenue' overstates the signal because Q1 still produced a net loss, and Q2 at higher revenue ($22.75B) reverted to an operating loss. The upside narrative should note that even the 'best' recent quarter was net-loss-making.
corrections.required_action: No score change needed; this is a framing nuance. The $461M operating income figure is factually correct per the raw pack. The PM should note that operating profit did not translate to net profit in Q1 2025.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'The improvement in equity from -$23.56B to -$3.30B over three quarters likely reflects accounting adjustments rather than organic earnings generation, given continued net losses.' This is speculative and not supported by any evidence in the raw packs. The raw packs contain no discussion of accounting adjustments, reclassifications, or non-cash items that would explain the $20.26B equity improvement. The report should either cite a source or flag this as unverifiable.
corrections.required_action: Remove or rephrase the speculation about 'accounting adjustments.' The equity improvement is a factual observation from the balance sheet, but the causal attribution is unsupported.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Boeing's operational recovery is real but fragile—Q2 2025 revenue hit $22.75B (+34.9% YoY) and the FAA is easing restrictions, yet the stock at $215.83 has already rallied 39.6% over the past year while TTM net losses remain $10.68B and equity is negative $3.30B. The risk/reward is roughly balanced: meaningful upside exists if margin normalization continues, but the stock has priced in much of the recovery narrative at a P/S of 2.17x with no earnings anchor.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Boeing's operational recovery is real but fragile—Q2 2025 revenue hit $22.75B (+34.9% YoY) and the FAA is easing restrictions, yet the stock at $215.83 has already rallied 39.6% over the past year while TTM net losses remain $10.68B and equity is negative $3.30B. The risk/reward is roughly balanced: meaningful upside exists if margin normalization continues, but the stock has priced in much of the recovery narrative at a P/S of 2.17x with no earnings anchor.
key_factors: Q2 2025 revenue of $22.75B (+34.9% YoY, +16.7% QoQ) confirms genuine delivery acceleration, but Q2 operating income reverted to -$176M loss after Q1's $461M profit, showing the margin trajectory is non-linear and unreliable.

Stock has returned +39.6% over the past year to $215.83 (67.5% off 52-week low of $128.88), pricing in substantial recovery while TTM net income is -$10.68B, TTM gross profit is -$244M, and equity is -$3.30B—leaving limited asymmetry.

FAA restored partial final safety-check authority (2025-09-26) and plans to ease production restrictions, a key operational catalyst, but proposed $3.14M civil penalties and Air India crash lawsuit maintain regulatory and legal overhang.

Balance sheet remains technically insolvent with $53.14B debt and negative equity of -$3.30B, though equity improved from -$23.56B in Q3 2024—debt service and dilution risk persist without confirmed positive free cash flow.
